strength.title: Block 1 Foundation Strength
strength.stimulus: Build baseline lower and upper body strength to support race stations. Volume-focused with moderate intensity.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: Dynamic: leg swings, hip circles, thoracic rotations, walking lunges x 10
2 sets bodyweight squats x 10, push-ups x 8

structure.heading: Bench Press (15 min)
items: Pair with pull-ups 2-3 sets x max reps (or banded)

structure.heading: Posterior Chain Circuit (15 min)
items: Single-leg glute bridge x 12 each side

structure.heading: Warm-up (5 min)
items: Dynamic: leg swings, hip circles x 8 each, 5 squats, 5 push-ups

notes.about: First strength day of the block. We're establishing your movement foundation — squat pattern, press pattern, hinge pattern. These three patterns carry you through every Hyrox station. RPE 6-7 means you have 3-4 reps in the tank. Today is about quality reps, not maxing out. If you haven't squatted in a while, start lighter than you think and build up across sets. Movement cues: Back squat — brace your core, knees track over toes. Bench press — feet planted, shoulder blades retracted. Romanian deadlift — soft knees, hinge from hips, feel the hamstrings.
notes.week_context: Welcome to Week 1 of Block 1 — this is your ACCUMULATION week. We're building volume with moderate intensity to lay the foundation. Expect higher reps, lower loads, and a focus on movement quality. Your body is adapting to the training stimulus. You should finish workouts feeling worked, not destroyed.
notes.block_context: Each 4-week block follows a pattern: Week 1 builds volume (Accumulation), Week 2 adds intensity (Intensification), Week 3 pushes peak performance (Peak), Week 4 is reduced volume for recovery (Deload). This structure prevents burnout and drives real progress.

running.title: Aerobic Base
running.stimulus: Build aerobic engine with easy conversational running. All zone 2 — no hard efforts this week.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: 10 min easy jog, gradually building pace
Dynamic drills last 2 min: A-skips, B-skips, strides x 3

structure.heading: Main Session (45 min)
items: Conversational pace — you should be able to hold a full conversation
If pace feels hard, slow down — this is base-building

structure.heading: Cool-down (5 min)
items: Light stretching: hamstrings, hip flexors, calves

notes.about: Base weeks are about building aerobic capacity. Zone 2 running (easy, conversational pace) improves your body's ability to use fat for fuel, increases capillary density in your muscles, and strengthens your heart. This is the foundation that lets you push harder later. Don't skip the easy days — they're what make the hard days possible. If you're unsure what zone 2 feels like: you can carry on a conversation but would prefer shorter sentences.
notes.week_context: Accumulation week — all easy running, no hard intervals. We'll add intensity next week. Focus on time on feet and consistent pacing.
notes.block_context: Running progression over 4 weeks: Week 1 all easy, Week 2 introduces tempo work, Week 3 adds race-pace intervals, Week 4 deload with one short tempo.

engine.title: Station Familiarization
engine.stimulus: Learn Hyrox station movements at moderate pace. Focus on technique, transitions, and pacing — not maximum intensity.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: 5 min easy row or ski erg
Dynamic: walking lunges, arm circles, torso twists

notes.about: Engine work for Hyrox isn't about going all-out every session. Today we're introducing the station movements at a moderate pace so your body learns the patterns. Wall balls: full squat depth, aim for consistent rhythm. Burpee broad jumps: stand fully, jump with intent, land soft. Transitions matter — at the race, the time you save moving efficiently between stations adds up. Practice that today.
notes.week_context: Accumulation week — introducing you to the stations without smashing you. Moderate pace, focus on technique.
notes.block_context: Engine work progression: Week 1 familiarization at moderate pace, Week 2 increases pace and reduces rest, Week 3 race-pace work, Week 4 reduced volume.

emom.title: Foundation EMOM
emom.stimulus: Build movement efficiency and pacing discipline through structured EMOM (Every Minute On the Minute) work.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: Dynamic warm-up: arm circles, leg swings, 10 air squats, 10 push-ups

structure.heading: EMOM 24 min
items: Aim to finish each minute with 10-20 seconds of rest

notes.about: EMOM work teaches you to pace. If you push too hard early, you won't finish the minute in time. If you go too easy, you're wasting the stimulus. The goal is finishing each work minute with 10-20 seconds of rest — that's the sweet spot. Over weeks, your body learns this rhythm and you start racing smarter, not just harder.
notes.week_context: Accumulation week — moderate work rates, focus on consistent pacing across all 6 rounds. If you can't finish a minute with rest, scale reps down.
notes.block_context: EMOM progression: Week 1 establishes the pattern, Week 2 increases reps, Week 3 peaks intensity, Week 4 reduces volume.

simulation.title: Intro Simulation
simulation.stimulus: First taste of compromised running + station work at moderate intensity. Goal is learning the feel, not racing.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: Dynamic: leg swings, lunges, strides x 3

structure.heading: Main Session (40 min)
items: 50m sled push (moderate weight, focus on form)
This is NOT race pace — it's learning pace

notes.about: Welcome to your first simulation. 'Compromised running' means running with tired legs from station work — this is the core challenge of Hyrox. Today we're at moderate pace because we're teaching your body the transitions. Later in the block we'll ramp up intensity. Key learnings: how your legs feel leaving a station, how quickly your breath settles, how to find a sustainable pace between stations.
notes.week_context: Accumulation week — introductory simulation at moderate pace. Don't race this. Learn the patterns.
notes.block_context: Simulation progression: Week 1 moderate pace familiarization, Week 2 threshold work, Week 3 race-pace simulation, Week 4 reduced volume tune-up.

notes.about: Week 1 of your 4-week training block. All sessions at moderate intensity to build foundation.
notes.week_context: Accumulation: volume-focused, moderate intensity, movement quality.
notes.block_context: This block runs Apr 6 - May 3. Progressive overload across 4 weeks leading to a deload in week 4.
